title: Chapter 2546: Perfected Heavenly Refinement
content:
Chapter 2546: Perfected Heavenly Refinement

"Enough of your farce. Follow me. I am going to check your identity again," Officer Mei said to Yuan before bringing him back to the previous room to collect his blood with the machine again.

This time, there was no error, and Officer Mei could finally see Yuan’s identity.

[Name: Yuan]

[Age: ???]

[Cultivation: Third Level First Ascension]

[Physique: Perfected Heavenly Refinement]

[Credential Rank: 1]

"?!?!?"

Officer Mei’s eyes widened with shock after seeing Yuan’s information on the machine.

She rubbed her eyes a moment before looking again, as if hoping to see something different, but everything remained the same.

Although Yuan’s age being unknown was puzzling and his low cultivation was baffling, they were nothing compared to his physique.

"Perfected Heavenly Refinement?! Impossible!" she cried out loud.

"What?" Yuan raised an eyebrow at this new term.

Officer Mei turned to look at Yuan’s puzzled face and swallowed nervously.

"H-How did you achieve Perfected Heavenly Refinement?" she asked him.

"What is that?" Yuan asked, still with an eyebrow raised.

"Stop messing with me already!" she shouted in anger.

Yuan frowned. "I really am not. I have no idea what this Perfected Heavenly Refinement is."

"It is your physique!"

"Uh... Even if you ask me that, how am I supposed to explain it? And even if I could explain it, what would be the point when you do not possess the physique?"

"What nonsense are you spewing? Of course, I have the same physique," Officer Mei responded with a frown on her face.

"What?" Yuan wore a dumbfounded look after hearing her expected words.

"You... also have the Heaven Refining Physique? I thought only one person could possess it at any given moment?"

Officer Mei shook her head and sighed, "There must really be something wrong with you. Acting like you’re the only one in this world with that physique when everyone in this world possesses the Heavenly Refinement physique."

"Everyone?!" Yuan exclaimed in disbelief. "I don’t believe you. How can everyone possess such a powerful physique?"

Officer Mei shrugged, "Whether you believe it or not won’t change reality. Whatever. Don’t tell me if you don’t want to."

"..."

Yuan fell silent.

’Everyone in this world possesses the Heaven Refining Physique...? What the hell is going on?’

Suddenly—

<Lockdown has ended>

<You may return to the surface>

"It ended quite swiftly this time..." Officer Mei mumbled after seeing the same notification.

Yuan looked at her and asked, "You can also see the notifications?"

He already had a feeling that would be the case, but needed confirmation.

"Isn’t it obvious? Everyone registered in the database has access to Gaia’s System."

Yuan then asked, "Who is Gaia? Are they the Eternal who created this world?"

"Huh? What nonsense are you spewing now? Eternal? What is that?"

’Gaia isn’t an Eternal? Or maybe they’re not aware of their existence...’

"Never mind..." Yuan shook his head before asking, "So, what now?"

Officer Mei narrowed her eyes at him.

A moment later, she spoke, "Since the lockdown has ended, everyone will be returning to the surface soon. As for you... Although it’s a crime not to register in the database, there’s clearly something wrong with you... mentally. Therefore, instead of putting you in jail, I’ll recommend you for psychiatric care. Consider yourself lucky."

"Psychiatric care...?" Yuan’s jaw dropped, as he could never have imagined being put in such a position.

<Officer Mei has recommended you for Psychiatric Care>

<You must visit a Psychiatrist within 14 days or face penalties>

"Done. You may leave now."

"Just like that? But I still have some questions for you," Yuan said.

"If you have any questions, ask your psychiatrist. If you linger around any longer, I’ll throw you in jail."

With no other choice, Yuan left the room. By the time he stepped outside the station, the once-crowded streets had become completely empty, and the underground city suddenly looked like a ghost town.

"How do I return to the surface?" Yuan mumbled.

Ding.

<Return to the surface now?>

When Yuan saw the notification, he wondered if the system would teleport him back to the surface.

He tapped on ’Yes’.

<Initializing Teleportation>

<Teleporting in 3...2...1>

Yuan’s vision blurred for an instant, and before he realized, he was back inside the building he had entered with the children before arriving at the underground shelter.

"...What now?"

He still had many questions, especially about Gaia’s System.

However, he didn’t know where to start.

Then, he looked at a certain notification that wouldn’t disappear.

<Visit a Psychiatrist>

<Time Limit: 13 days, 23 hours, 55 minutes, 27 seconds>

"It’s almost like a quest... Do I really have to visit one?" he sighed out loud, wondering what sort of penalties he’d face if he disobeyed it.

"System, where can I find a Psychiatrist?" he then asked, hoping the system could hear and answer him.

<Locating nearest Psychiatrist>

<Found Psychiatrist>

<Begin Direction Assistance?>

"Wow, this thing is pretty convenient..." Yuan stared at the notifications with awe.

"Begin."

The next moment, a neon-green line appeared on the ground before him, presumably guiding him toward his destination.

When he looked closely, it even showed him the distance and time required to reach it.

Thus, Yuan started following the system’s directions.

As he started following the neon-green light, Yuan realized that the gravity in this world was no longer affecting him as much.

"This world really resembles the Earth I know, except being countless times more advanced..."

After reaching the end of the street and arriving at an intersection, a bright red ’Stop’ notification appeared before him.

Then, figures began soaring past Yuan along the road.

However, they were not riding ordinary vehicles. Instead, they stood atop what appeared to be hoverboards, surfing through the sky at incredible speeds.

"Mother, why is that person’s aura so weak? Isn’t he an adult? Is there something wrong with him?"

A nearby child suddenly spoke aloud, prompting Yuan to turn instinctively toward the voice, where he saw a little boy pointing directly at him.

"It’s rude to point!"

The boy’s mother quickly pressed his arm down before turning to face Yuan with an apologetic smile.

"..."

’Even a little kid like that is a Cultivation God...’ Yuan sighed inwardly.

Suddenly, he realized that he had not checked his status yet.

"Show status."

[Name: Yuan]

[Age: ???]

[Cultivation: Third Level First Ascension]

[Physique: Perfected Heavenly Refinement]

[Credential Rank: 1]

’Third Level First Ascension?’ Yuan raised an eyebrow at his cultivation.

’Is that what God Ascension is called in this world?’

Then he saw his physique, and sure enough, it was called Perfected Heavenly Refinement.

’Credential Rank... this must be what I need to raise in order to cure my other curses... but how do I raise it?’

Ding!

<In order to raise your Credential Rank, you must contribute to society>

’Contribute to society...?’

"Hm?"

Before Yuan realized it, he had reached what appeared to be a dead end.

A tall wall stood before him, with a single entrance guarded by several people. Above the entrance, the number ’10’ was marked.

"How do I get past?" Yuan asked one of the guards.

The guard pointed to the machine standing nearby and said, "We will need to check your credentials."

Yuan approached the machine, which resembled the one inside the station that examined his identity, and placed his hand into the slot.

A moment later, he felt a drop of his blood being collected.

Di.

A green light appeared on the machine.

"You may pass," said the guard as he opened the door.

Yuan didn’t question it and passed through the door.

<You have entered Sector 10>

Yuan continued following the green line on the ground, and after another half an hour of walking, he reached a large building that was clearly a hospital.

"..."

After staring at it for a long moment, Yuan reluctantly entered the building and approached the front desk.

"I was recommended to see a psychiatrist," he said to the woman behind the desk.

Without a word, the woman pointed to the small machine on top of the desk.

Sure enough, it was another one of those identity-reading machines.

’Do I really have to give my blood every time I need to do something in this world?’ he sighed inwardly as he placed his hand into the slot, feeding it a drop of his blood.

"Take a seat. We’ll call you when it’s your turn," said the woman.

Yuan went to the waiting area and waited for his turn. In the meantime, he continued testing the system’s capabilities.

"System, tell me about this world."

<This world is called Earth>

"Anything else?"

<User lacks the credentials>

’So even information requires credentials, huh?’

"Can you tell me about the reason we have to hide in the underground shelter?"

<User lacks the credentials>

Right as Yuan was about to ask another question—

Ding!

<Your meeting with a psychiatrist has been approved. Head to Room 221 as soon as possible>

"Already?"

Even though he had just sat down, Yuan didn’t complain and immediately made his way to room 221.

Upon arriving at his destination, Yuan knocked on the closed door.

"You can enter."

A female voice quickly responded.

When Yuan opened the door and stepped into the room, he was met by another familiar face.

"Wang Xiuying?" he muttered with a dazed expression.

"Hm?" The woman who resembled Wang Xiuying raised an eyebrow at him but didn’t respond.

A moment later, she gestured at the chair before her and said, "Please, take a seat."

Once he was seated, the woman said, "I am your psychiatrist, Doctor Ying. According to your referrer, Officer Mei, you believe that you are from another world. Can you tell me why you believe you are from another world?"
